title: Choosing a Variant for a Task
concept: Choose BERT for tagging/classification, GPT for text generation, and T5 for translation. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Practical NLP Tasks.'

exercises.prompt: Why do we have different model variants like BERT, GPT, and T5?

options.text: They are built differently for different types of tasks.
They are all exactly the same.
They only work in different languages.

exercises.explain: Different AI models are pre-trained with different goals in mind, such as understanding text vs. generating text.

cards.title: The Right Tool for the Job
cards.body: Imagine hiring a chef to fix your plumbing—it's the wrong fit! AI models are the same. Picking the right model variant (BERT, GPT, or T5) makes your natural language processing project a success.

cards.title: Matching Model to Task
cards.body: Choosing a variant means matching your task to the model's architecture. BERT looks at words on both sides to understand context (perfect for classification). GPT looks forward to predict the next word (ideal for writing). T5 treats every task as a text translation or transformation.

cards.title: The AI Work Office
cards.body: Think of NLP models like specialists in a workspace. BERT is like a scanner who categorizes documents, GPT is like an imaginative writer drafting stories, and T5 is like a translator converting documents from one language to another.

cards.title: AI Roles in Action
cards.body: If you want to build a spam filter that labels emails as 'Spam' or 'Inbox', you choose BERT. If you want a chatbot that writes personalized emails, you choose GPT. If you need to turn English questions into French answers, you choose T5.

cards.title: One Size Does Not Fit All
cards.body: Some people think one powerful AI model can easily do every single language task perfectly. In reality, choosing a specialized variant saves computer power, runs faster, and gets much more accurate results for your specific task!

cards.title: BERT vs. GPT
cards.body: Different model architectures are optimized for different types of work. Let's compare their best uses:

left.text: Perfect for tagging, labeling, and classifying text.

right.text: Perfect for writing, chatting, and text generation.

exercises.prompt: Which model variant should you choose if you want to classify customer reviews as positive or negative?
exercises.explain: BERT reads sentences in both directions to fully grasp their meaning, making it excellent for sorting and classification.

exercises.prompt: T5 is a great model choice when you need to translate text from English to Spanish.
exercises.explain: T5 stands for Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer and is designed to translate and transform text from one format to another.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to match the model to its ideal task.
exercises.sentence: While BERT is great for classification, GPT is the best choice for text [blank].
exercises.explain: GPT is built to predict the next word, making it the perfect tool for writing and generating text.

exercises.prompt: Which of these statements correctly identifies the best model for writing a blog post?

options.text: BERT: It excels at predicting the next word in a story.
GPT: It is built for text generation and writing tasks.
T5: It is only capable of classifying text, not writing it.

exercises.explain: GPT is designed to generate new, creative text, making it the right choice for drafting articles.

exercises.prompt: When picking an NLP model variant for a new project, what is the most important rule to remember?

options.text: BERT is the best choice for writing creative fiction from scratch.
We match the model's design (BERT, GPT, T5) to the specific requirements of our task.
T5 is only used for sorting emails into folders.
GPT is primarily designed for translation and nothing else.

exercises.explain: Choosing the correct model variant ensures you have the right tool for the job. BERT categorizes, GPT writes, and T5 translates. Next up: Practical NLP Tasks.
